creator: ecki@zusammenkunft.net
text:
Hm, the file in question was created just a moment before, and there are no dmesg warnings along the line.

The bug is also reproduceable, if you let the 0byte file stand around with the same (broken) server it will time out again, but if you use a working server it will get this bad file descriptor. I just tested the file is perfectly functional:

// readable:
# cat f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
// appendable
# echo bla >> f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
// still readabke
# cat f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
bla
// Even when i create it manually it happens:
# : > f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
# rm -rf .fetch*
# portsnap -s isc.portsnap.freebsd.org fetch
Looking up isc.portsnap.freebsd.org mirrors... none found.
Fetching snapshot tag from isc.portsnap.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching snapshot metadata... done.
Fetching snapshot generated at Wed Jan 28 01:15:18 CET 2015:
f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0  0% of   70 MB    0  Bps
fetch: http://isc.portsnap.freebsd.org/s/f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz: Bad file descriptor
fetch: f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz: Bad file descriptor
# ls -la
total 112
drwxr-xr-x   2 root  wheel    512 Jan 28 21:54 .
drwxr-xr-x  21 root  wheel   1024 Jan 28 21:09 ..
-rw-r--r--   1 root  wheel      0 Jan 28 01:34 .fetch.4EtwkP.f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
-rw-r--r--   1 root  wheel      0 Jan 28 21:29 f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz

Looks like the proble happens before downloading the content into the temp file, I tried to truss the fetch command used, but it does not contain a ERR#9 (EBADF). hm? (http://bernd.eckenfels.net/view/fetch.truss)

The problem does not happen if the file contains any ("bla") bytes. I suspect its a mmap/flock thing? 

The machine is about to be replaced, that is why it is an old base system and I did not file a tool bug. But it looks like broken mirrors cause reports every now and then.
